method: Exhaustive threshold sweep on A2P scores (train==test setup)
key_finding: Oracle threshold search gives F1=43.58% - only 0.48pp above A2P actual (43.1%). A2P already uses near-optimal threshold (99pct). The score distribution itself is the bottleneck, not the threshold. Maximum achievable F1 with these scores is ~43.6%, far below paper paper claim of 67.55%. This means ~24pp gap is fundamental and cannot be closed by threshold tuning.
implication_for_paper_gap: Our 43.1% (train==test) vs paper 67.55%: gap of 24pp is NOT from threshold choice. It is from data source difference (paper uses proper SVDB data). The paper result of 67.55% requires genuine out-of-sample generalization.
